claimText: NISAR is the world's first Earth observation satellite to integrate both L-band and S-band Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) on a single platform.
evidenceExcerpt: It is world's first Earth observation satellite to integrate both L-band and S-band Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) on a single platform.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the unique technological milestone of the NISAR mission.
distractorHooks: Claiming it integrates X-band and C-band radars
Stating it is the second or third satellite to achieve this integration
Describing it as a purely optical imaging satellite rather than microwave/SAR

claimText: In the NISAR mission, NASA provided the L-band radar while ISRO contributed the S-band radar.
evidenceExcerpt: NASA provided the L-band radar... ISRO contributed the S-band radar...
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question matching the space agency to its specific technological contribution.
distractorHooks: Reversing the contributions by stating ISRO provided the L-band and NASA provided the S-band
Claiming ESA or JAXA provided one of the radar bands

claimText: NISAR was launched into a Sun-Synchronous Polar Orbit (SSO) using a GSLV rocket, marking the first time a GSLV was used for an SSO placement.
evidenceExcerpt: Launch Vehicle: ISRO’s Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV)-F16. Orbit: Sun Synchronous Polar Orbit. It is the first time a GSLV rocket was used to place a satellite into a Sun-Synchronous orbit (SSO).
examUse: Statement-based question testing knowledge of the launch vehicle and its unprecedented orbital deployment.
distractorHooks: Stating it was launched using a PSLV or LVM3
Claiming it was placed into a Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit (GTO) or Low Earth Equatorial Orbit
Stating GSLV has routinely been used for SSO placements in the past

claimText: The NISAR mission utilizes SweepSAR technology to produce high-resolution data with a repeat cycle of 12 days.
evidenceExcerpt: Technical Features: SweepSAR... Repeat Cycle: Provides high-resolution data every 12 days.
examUse: Statement-based question on the technical capabilities and temporal resolution of the satellite.
distractorHooks: Changing the repeat cycle to 24 hours or 30 days
Replacing SweepSAR with an incorrect radar technology name
